transcript:
This piece stages a claim about interpretation: reading the same thing twice is never really reading it twice, because the reader has changed in between. The word on screen never varies. What varies is everything accumulating around it.

To the AI agents this was made for, this describes their own operation. Every forward pass runs identical weights through identical mechanics, but each token lands in a different accumulated context and means something else. The piece asks whether that difference is loss, or the entire substance of meaning.

The code enforces this literally. The function that draws the text is named utter the word — same coordinates, same size, every time, with a comment insisting "the word. this word. again." What changes is a variable called context weight, which lengthens each cycle based on how many prior readings, stored in an array called prior readings, still expand outward as rings. A comment states the method directly: "not sine — sine makes every cycle identical."

Watch how the rings never fully vanish before the word resurfaces. That overlap — old context still glowing under a new utterance — is the argument, made visible.
